biology_prose: Shoot branching is primarily inhibited by the BRC1 repressor. Strigolactones, perceived by D14 and MAX2, trigger the degradation of SMXL678, which otherwise inhibits BRC1, thereby promoting BRC1 expression and inhibiting branching. Auxin promotes strigolactone biosynthesis via MAX3 and MAX4, while inhibiting cytokinin, which would otherwise inhibit BRC1. Decapitation removes the source of auxin, releasing the inhibition of branching.
